Your task is to: put some toiletpaper on garbagecan. | +## Retrieved Relevant Experience | +### General Principles | +- **Plan with Landmarks**: Continuously update and reference a map of known object and container locations to choose shortest paths and prevent aimless wandering. | +- **Open Before Judging Empty**: Treat any closed container as unexplored; open it and inspect contents before deciding that the goal object is absent. | +- **Use State-Changing Tools Early**: For tasks requiring heated/cooled/clean objects, acquire the object, then immediately use the nearest suitable appliance to change its state before any placement. | +- **Destination First Policy**: After picking up a goal object, navigate directly to the known target receptacle and place it before resuming further search. | +- **Systematic Exploration**: Search every plausible surface or container exactly once before revisiting; prioritize unopened or unseen locations to cover the whole room methodically. | +- **Track Counts & Progress**: Maintain an internal counter of how many goal objects are still needed; stop searching or terminate episode only when the counter reaches zero. | +### Task-Relevant Skills | +- **Sink First for Cleaning**: Upon holding the target object, go straight to the nearest sink, basin, or faucet and issue the clean/use command before any placement attempts. | +_Apply when: Once the target object is in hand and its required state is ‘clean’._ | +- **Direct Post-Cooling Delivery**: Once the object is cooled, navigate straight to the destination and place it without extraneous detours or re-searching. | +_Apply when: Right after the cooling action succeeds._ | +- **No Appliance Before Object**: Do not move to or interact with the microwave or final placement location until the target object is picked up, preventing wasted navigation steps. | +_Apply when: Whenever you are tempted to head to microwave/destination without holding the required object._ | +- **Place Before More Search**: When holding any goal object and the target location is known and reachable, navigate there and place it immediately, then resume searching if more items are needed. | +_Apply when: While carrying a required object and the destination has been identified._ | +- **Prevent Action Loops**: Avoid toggling the same lamp or re-examining the same empty spot; if no progress after one full check, relocate to a new plausible lamp location. | +_Apply when: When repeated identical actions yield no new observations or objects._ | +- **Single Toggle Rule**: Use (toggle) the desklamp only after the target is in hand or co-located; avoid repeated or premature toggles that waste steps. | +_Apply when: About to interact with a desklamp during an examine task._ | +### Mistakes to Avoid | +- **Don't**: Agent repeatedly revisits the same already-searched locations or containers instead of exploring new ones. | +**Instead**: Maintain an exploration map that records which rooms

+user | +You are an expert agent operating in the ALFRED Embodied Environment. Your task is to: put two soapbottle in garbagecan. | +## Retrieved Relevant Experience | +### General Principles | +- **Plan with Landmarks**: Continuously update and reference a map of known object and container locations to choose shortest paths and prevent aimless wandering. | +- **Destination First Policy**: After picking up a goal object, navigate directly to the known target receptacle and place it before resuming further search. | +- **Open Before Judging Empty**: Treat any closed container as unexplored; open it and inspect contents before deciding that the goal object is absent. | +- **Track Counts & Progress**: Maintain an internal counter of how many goal objects are still needed; stop searching or terminate episode only when the counter reaches zero. | +- **Use State-Changing Tools Early**: For tasks requiring heated/cooled/clean objects, acquire the object, then immediately use the nearest suitable appliance to change its state before any placement. | +- **Systematic Exploration**: Search every plausible surface or container exactly once before revisiting; prioritize unopened or unseen locations to cover the whole room methodically. | +### Task-Relevant Skills | +- **Sink First for Cleaning**: Upon holding the target object, go straight to the nearest sink, basin, or faucet and issue the clean/use command before any placement attempts. | +_Apply when: Once the target object is in hand and its required state is ‘clean’._ | +- **No Appliance Before Object**: Do not move to or interact with the microwave or final placement location until the target object is picked up, preventing wasted navigation steps. | +_Apply when: Whenever you are tempted to head to microwave/destination without holding the required object._ | +- **Confirm Object Match**: After picking up an item, verify it matches the requested type; if not, drop it and resume the search. | +_Apply when: Whenever you acquire an object but the goal object is still unmet._ | +- **Place Before More Search**: When holding any goal object and the target location is known and reachable, navigate there and place it immediately, then resume searching if more items are needed. | +_Apply when: While carrying a required object and the destination has been identified._ | +- **Phase-Ordered Plan**: Always execute clean tasks in the fixed sequence: 1) locate & acquire target object, 2) bring it to an available water source/sink to clean, 3) navigate to final location, 4) place object. | +_Apply when: As soon as the goal specifies the object must be clean before placement._ | +- **Direct Post-Cooling Delivery**: Once the object is cooled, navigate straight to the destination and place it without extraneous detours or re-searching. | +_Apply when: Right after the cooling action succeeds._ | +### Mistakes to Avoid | +- **Don't**: Agent repeatedly revisits the same already-searched locations or containers instead of exploring new ones. | +**Instead**: Maintain an exploration

open it and inspect contents before deciding that the goal object is absent. | +### Task-Relevant Skills | +- **Sink First for Cleaning**: Upon holding the target object, go straight to the nearest sink, basin, or faucet and issue the clean/use command before any placement attempts. | +_Apply when: Once the target object is in hand and its required state is ‘clean’._ | +- **Direct Post-Cooling Delivery**: Once the object is cooled, navigate straight to the destination and place it without extraneous detours or re-searching. | +_Apply when: Right after the cooling action succeeds._ | +- **Phase-Ordered Plan**: Always execute clean tasks in the fixed sequence: 1) locate & acquire target object, 2) bring it to an available water source/sink to clean, 3) navigate to final location, 4) place object. | +_Apply when: As soon as the goal specifies the object must be clean before placement._ | +- **Systematic Surface Search**: On each candidate lamp surface, look, open nearby containers/drawers once, then move on—ensuring all plausible lamp locations are covered. | +_Apply when: After the lamp at the current surface is on and the target hasn’t been found._ | +- **Prevent Action Loops**: Avoid toggling the same lamp or re-examining the same empty spot; if no progress after one full check, relocate to a new plausible lamp location. | +_Apply when: When repeated identical actions yield no new observations or objects._ | +- **Explore New Surfaces**: If either the target or the desklamp hasn’t been found, systematically check unvisited surfaces/containers instead of re-inspecting places already confirmed empty. | +_Apply when: Location of required object remains unknown after current area is exhausted._ | +### Mistakes to Avoid | +- **Don't**: Agent repeatedly revisits the same alread
